editorial.headline: All six wells contaminated in 2022; permanent treatment online
editorial.narrative: PFAS was found in all six municipal wells in early 2022. An interim resin-based anion exchange system came online with the new Drinking Water Treatment Facility in December 2022; a permanent ~$17M granular activated carbon (GAC) system replaced it in late 2024. The city sued 3M and other manufacturers in 2023 and joined the national AFFF settlement. Water rates rose roughly 65% with plant construction. GAC media reactivation contracts are now part of ongoing operating costs.

editorial.headline: Two wells offline in 2022; permanent GAC installed 2025
editorial.narrative: Weston took Wells 3 and 4 offline in 2022 after PFAS exceeded state health advisory levels. Temporary GAC let Well 4 resume pumping later that year. A permanent GAC system was installed in 2025 â€” Well 4 began pumping through it in October, with about half the ~$1.8M cost covered by principal forgiveness under the state's Safe Drinking Water Loan Program. Well 3 is expected back in service in 2026.

editorial.headline: PFAS in two of four wells; permanent plant targeted spring 2026
editorial.narrative: Rib Mountain found PFAS in two of its four municipal wells. Interim resin filtration on Well 1 has reduced treated water to non-detectable levels, but a January 2026 public notice confirmed the system still exceeded Wisconsin's hazard index guidance as of December 2025. A permanent treatment plant has been under construction since mid-2024.
editorial.treatment: Ion exchange resin (interim); permanent plant under construction

editorial.headline: PFAS above 2022 federal health advisories; pumping adjustments
editorial.narrative: Mosinee wells tested above EPA's 2022 interim health advisory levels. The city adjusted pumping between wells rather than installing treatment. Verify current approach before launch.

editorial.headline: Well shut down in 2022; new treatment facility
editorial.narrative: Rothschild shut down a municipal well after PFAS was detected in 2022 and moved forward with a new treatment facility expected complete by the end of 2025.
editorial.treatment: Under construction / recently completed â€” verify current status
